figures.caption: Fig. 3: Key frames from the shared-control experiment, showing the first-person VR view and the corresponding RViz visualization of the motion primitive planner’s evolution as the user interacts with the VR target to guide the aerial team.

Fig. 1: Mixed-reality experiment with one real and two simulated drones (white). The operator in the background guides the drones in the VR environment using a red marker (Fusr), and the planner reacts to this input in real-time (pink). pbar and pc are robots barycenter and migration point; Frep is the obstacle repulsion. Frames I,M,B,V,H are defined in Section III-A.

Fig. 4: Drone trajectories and multi-robots metrics for baseline and shared-control experiments. (a,b) Trajectories colored by user force Fusr. (c,d) Distance to obstacles di,obs. (e,f) Inter-agent distances dij . Smoothed over 50 samples.

Fig. 2: Overview of the system: navigation stack (left; topics include barycenter of the robots pbar, obstacle repulsive forces Frep,i, agent positions pi, migration point pc, and commanded positions pc,i), bidirectional WebSocket communication layer (center; topics detailed in Table I), and VR visualization of drones, local planner path, and environment (right).
